c0.q: When did the party who gained control of congress in the midterm elections in 1946 take control of the determiner of rules of the US House and US Senate?
c0.gen_cached:
The party that gained control of Congress in the 1946 midterm elections was the Republican Party. They took control of both the U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate in that election.

- The **House of Representatives**: The Republicans gained control of the House in the 1946 elections and took over the **determination of rules** (i.e., the leadership and procedural authority) of the House on **January 3, 1947**, when the 80th Congress convened.

- The **Senate**: Similarly, the Republicans gained control of the Senate in the 1946 elections and assumed leadership and rule-setting authority on **January 3, 1947**, when the 80th Congress began.

Thus, the Republican Party, which gained control in the 1946 midterms, took control of the determiner of rules (i.e., leadership and procedural authority) of both the U.S. House and Senate on **January 3, 1947**.

c25.q: When did the party who gained control of congress in the midterm elections in 1946 take control of the determiner of rules of the US House and US Senate?
c25.gen_cached:
The party that gained control of Congress in the 1946 midterm elections was the **Republican Party**.

They took control of the **U.S. House of Representatives** in the 1946 elections, and they also gained control of the **U.S. Senate** in the same elections.

### Key Points:
- **House of Representatives**: The Republicans gained a majority in the House in the 1946 elections, ending 16 years of Democratic control. This gave them control of the **House Rules Committee**, which determines the rules for debate and amendments on the House floor.
- **Senate**: The Republicans also gained control of the Senate in 1946, ending Democratic control. This gave them control of the **Senate Rules and Administration Committee**, which oversees Senate procedures and rules.

### Answer:
The Republican Party, which gained control of Congress in the 1946 midterm elections, took control of the **determiners of rules** in both chambers:
- **House Rules Committee** (House of Representatives) — **January 1947**
- **Senate Rules and Administration Committee** (Senate) — **January 1947**

Thus, the party that gained control in 1946 took control of the rule-making bodies of both the House and Senate **in January 1947**, at the start of the 80th Congress.
